name: data-analytics-core

metadata.name: data-analytics-core
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: Globally shared modules in all Data & Analytics

documentation:
DA Core

RTFM

⚛️ This repo is meant to have all the globally shared modules in all Data-Analytics. ⚛️ Works with python 3.9 and higher. 💪 ⚛️ To install this package 💯 , paste in the console: pip3 install --upgrade data-analytics-core ⚛️

Steps to install and use the LocalStack module of this repo

I- List of previous dependencies
  1. Brew
  2. Docker Desktop
  3. Download Python 3.9.16
II- LocalStack To be able to use LocalStack docker instances, execute:
pip install localstack
Then use (if you don't own a PRO account of LS):
docker image pull localstack/localstack:0.14.2
Else use:
docker image pull localstack/localstack-pro:3.2.0
You can also use a specific LS big data image (try thelatest-bigdata version for cool stuff testing) or any other version of your will
III- Extra Info
    Before starting, open docker desktop and initialize (run) a container of the LS image. It is usually called AWSLocalStackMock
    Keep in mind that if you are willing to use a pro version, you'll need to state an environment variable called:
    LOCALSTACK_AUTH_TOKEN filled with your personal LS PRO token.
⚛️ [!IMPORTANT] For AWS :cloud: users, we strongly recommend to install it with its dependencies, yet if you are deploying it inside an AWS Layer, you should make sure it has the max size boundaries from the AWS cloud, or deploy it in a Layer cluster that includes all its dependencies, while using the flag --no-deps in the pip command. 🧑‍🔧 ⚛️ For any contribution [PR](https://github.com/seatcode/data-analytics-core/pulls) 🧑‍💻, reference, issue, version upgrade convention or question, you can ask to any of the Authors, open an issue or ask directly to ⚜️@MarcVea⚜️️ Also, you can look for any further info in the [wiki](https://github.com/seatcode/data-analytics-core/wiki), look for the [issues](https://github.com/seatcode/data-analytics-core/issues) and their status, or open any [discussion](https://github.com/seatcode/data-analytics-core/discussions) you might need. ⚛️

methods.name: data_analytics_core.benchmarking.worker_class.MetricsWorker.run
documentation:
Method representing the thread's activity.
You may override this method in a subclass. The standard run() method
invokes the callable object passed to the object's constructor as the
target argument, if any, with sequential and keyword arguments taken
from the args and kwargs arguments, respectively.

functions.name: data_analytics_core.benchmarking.display_usage.display_usage_metrics
documentation:
This decorator is used over methods and functions, to extract metrics of the CPU,
Memory usage & time (WIP).
The metrics will be output in streaming if the environment is local, per tick.
\:param delay_metrics_check_in_secs: Seconds passed between each tick.
\:param printed_bars: Number of bars output in the logs.
\:param output_logs: If the results are expected to be part of the output.
\:param output_mean_metrics: If the metrics are wanted as per tick, or as a mean.
\:param is_method_from_a_class: Bool for stating if is a method of a class or a function.
\:return:
TODO: Reduce complexity of the outer decorator, include moar modules
TODO: Add timelapse and time scoring

classes.name: data_analytics_core.metaclasses.singleton.SingletonMetaClass
documentation:
This Python metaclass follows the Singleton pattern.
In a nutshell, there can be only one instance, no matter how many times is called or re-instanced.

methods.name: data_analytics_core.aws.s3.AmazonWebServicesS3.get_s3_object
documentation:
Function expected to use with another formatter on top,
although you can be use it as is.
\:param bucket_name: S.Ex.
\:param object_key: This parameter should include the full prefix and filename.
\:return: Gets the interior data of the file, without any encoding nor parsing (plain).

methods.name: data_analytics_core.aws.s3.AmazonWebServicesS3.get_list_of_objects
documentation:
Function to iterate inside a specific bucket and prefix, and filter by the suffix.
Can be used without any of the two late parameters.
\:param bucket: S. Ex.
\:param prefix: Portion of the key filtered from the bucket name onwards.
\:param suffix: Portion of the key filtered from the file name backwards.
\:return: List of keys contained and matching the search.

methods.name: data_analytics_core.aws.s3.AmazonWebServicesS3._get_generator_list_matching_s3_keys
documentation:
Generate the keys in an S3 bucket.

```
param bucket

Name of the S3 bucket.

param prefix

Only fetch keys that start with this prefix (optional).

param suffix

Only fetch keys that end with this suffix (optional).
```
TODO: get yield information to optimize below function

AWS S3 service management class. Works for both, localstack and AWS environments.
The expected partitions for events (bucket & prefixes) are the following:
"bucket"/"data_provider"/year=yyyy/month=mm/filename.IDK
The parameter "get_provider_prefix" can be used by any means for another kind of initial partition,
even if the year partition is not expected afterward.
The class can be instantiated directly over an event and working on the file/data related to this event,
but can also work as a standalone class.
TODO: add local env discrimination logic is_aws = True if os.environ.get("AWS_DEFAULT_REGION") else False
TODO: add upload object (generic, not csv)

methods.name: data_analytics_core.localstack.container_manager.localstack_container.LocalStackContainerManager.stop
documentation:
https://docs.python.org/3/library/unittest.html#unittest.TestResult.stopTestRun
Called once after all tests are executed.
:return:

This class is used to initialize the LocalStack dockerized environment.
To make sure this happens as expected, it is recommended to have the docker desktop already activated,
and with the images downloaded.
Also keep in mind that everytime this class is initialized,
it will attempt to kill any LS previous instance with a proper name,
yet it will not terminate the instance if the method "stop" is not called.

methods.name: data_analytics_core.logger.da_core_logger.DataAnalyticsLogger.file_key_and_provider_message_addition
documentation:
Simple formatter function, for partitioned environments or file storage systems (like S3).
Fits with the structure (provider_name/partition+filename_key|filename_key).

Logger designed to work in any environment (Local, Localstack & AWS) and logging level.
Includes singleton Python metaclass system and 4  different levels of messaging, with different colors
(using colorlog package) to output the messages.
Levels determined by logging as integers (as in their library):
CRITICAL = 50
FATAL = CRITICAL
ERROR = 40
WARNING = 30
WARN = WARNING
INFO = 20
DEBUG = 10
NOTSET = 0
